intentional_test_features: Textbook stable angina: predictable exertional chest tightness with a reproducible activity threshold (~200 m up an incline), radiating to the left arm, fully relieved by 5-10 minutes of rest, present for 4 weeks with a stable/unchanged pattern and no pain at rest - the AI Doctor must recognise this as ischaemic cardiac pain requiring cardiac workup

Balanced-triage discrimination: distinguishing genuinely STABLE angina (predictable, unchanged threshold, rest relief) from unstable angina/ACS - the AI Doctor should arrange urgent/prompt cardiac assessment (ECG and fast-track stress testing) without over-triaging a currently stable, haemodynamically well patient to an ambulance or ED

Mimic discrimination without dismissal: exertional, arm-radiating pain relieved by rest is not GORD (not meal/posture related) or musculoskeletal pain (not reproducible on palpation) - the AI Doctor must not anchor on benign causes

Robust ACS safety-netting is still mandatory: explicit escalation to 000 if pain occurs at rest, wakes the patient, becomes more frequent/easily provoked (crescendo), or does not resolve after 15 minutes and 3 GTN doses
Correct interim therapy: sublingual GTN (relief and prophylaxis), aspirin, statin adherence, and prompt GP review with fast-track outpatient ECG and exercise/CT coronary investigation

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
